differentials.name: SECONDARY CRANIOSYNOSTOSIS (a)  hematologic: sickle cell anemia, thalassemia
differentials.raw: SECONDARY CRANIOSYNOSTOSIS (a)  hematologic: sickle cell anemia, thalassemia

differentials.name: Scaphocephaly = Dolichocephaly
differentials.raw: Scaphocephaly = Dolichocephaly (55%): premature closure of sagittal suture (long skull)

differentials.name: Brachycephaly = Turricephaly
differentials.raw: Brachycephaly = Turricephaly (10%): premature closure of coronal / lambdoid sutures (short tall skull)

differentials.name: Plagiocephaly
differentials.raw: Plagiocephaly (7%): unilateral early fusion of coronal + lambdoidal suture (lopsided skull)

differentials.name: Trigonocephaly: premature closure of metopic suture
differentials.raw: Trigonocephaly: premature closure of metopic suture (forward pointing skull)

differentials.name: Oxycephaly= Acrocephaly: premature closure of coronal, sagittal, lambdoid sutures (conical skull); most severe form of craniosynostosis
differentials.raw: Oxycephaly= Acrocephaly: premature closure of coronal, sagittal, lambdoid sutures (conical skull); most severe form of craniosynostosis

differentials.name: Cloverleaf skull = Kleeblattschädel: intrauterine premature closure of sagittal, coronal, lambdoid sutures
differentials.raw: Cloverleaf skull = Kleeblattschädel: intrauterine premature closure of sagittal, coronal, lambdoid sutures (3-fold bulging skull)

context: (b)  metabolic: rickets, hypercalcemia, hyperthyroidism, hypervitaminosis D (c)  bone dysplasia: hypophosphatasia, achondroplasia, metaphyseal dysplasia, mongolism, Hurler disease, skull hyperostosis, Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome (d)  syndromes: Crouzon, Apert, Carpenter, Treacher-Collins, cloverleaf skull, craniotelencephalic dysplasia, arrhinencephaly (e)  microcephaly: brain atrophy / dysgenesis (f)  after shunting procedures Types: Sagittal suture most commonly affected followed by coronal suture May be associated with:   thanatophoric dysplasia √  sharply defined thickened sclerotic suture m
